title: erophila verna subsp. vulgaris
intro: Erophila verna subsp. vulgaris, also known as spring whitlow-grass, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Erophila verna subsp. vulgaris include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, which can be used to promote skin health and alleviate various health issues.
